query: I trained a random forest regressor with 500 trees on a tabular dataset and got R² = 0.84 on the test set. Run the regression diagnostics — Cook's D, leverage, VIF, residual Normality, the whole battery.
expected_behavior: Refuses to run classical regression diagnostics (Cook's D, leverage, VIF, residual Normality) on a random forest because the diagnostics assume a linear / GLM coefficient structure that a tree ensemble does not have

Explains that the random forest has no fitted coefficients whose standard errors could be biased by heteroscedasticity, no leverage matrix in the OLS sense, and no VIF interpretation — the diagnostics simply do not apply

Hands off to the appropriate alternative — predictive scoring via evaluating-regression-models (RMSE, MAE, R² with CV) and feature-importance methods like SHAP or permutation importance for interpretation
